name: Andy Griffith
blurb: The pre-Carlin, pre-Cosby pioneer of the long comic monologue — 'What It Was, Was Football' in 1953 essentially invented the modern stand-up record.
bio: Andy Griffith is the proto-stand-up of the LP era: a country preacher persona, 'Deacon Andy Griffith,' delivering a single seven-minute story straight into a microphone before anyone called that stand-up. 'What It Was, Was Football' — recorded live at a Jefferson Standard insurance convention in September 1953, released on Colonial Records that November, reissued by Capitol in December and a top-10 Billboard hit by 1954 — is the conceptual ancestor of every long-form comic monologue that followed, from Newhart's telephone bits to Cosby's Wonderfulness to Pryor's neighborhood epics. He kept building the form on Capitol with 'Romeo and Juliet,' 'Hamlet,' 'Opera Carmen,' and 'Conversations With a Mule,' Shakespeare and grand opera retold by a rural narrator who has missed the entire point. Then the wider world arrived: A Face in the Crowd (1957, Kazan) gave him a feral dramatic performance still cited as one of the great American screen debuts; No Time for Sergeants (Broadway and film) gave him stardom; The Andy Griffith Show (1960–68) and Matlock (1986–95) gave him Mayberry and a second act. The stand-up canon usually starts with Mort Sahl and Lenny Bruce. Griffith was there first — just in a different costume.

timeline.ev: Born 1 June in Mount Airy, North Carolina
Graduates UNC Chapel Hill (music); begins touring as a monologist on the Carolina Playmakers / chautauqua circuit
Records 'What It Was, Was Football' live at a Jefferson Standard insurance convention in September; released November on Colonial Records as 'Deacon Andy Griffith'
Capitol Records reissues 'What It Was, Was Football' — reaches No. 9 on the Billboard pop chart; B-side 'Romeo and Juliet' becomes the second canonical monologue
Stars in the teleplay No Time for Sergeants on The United States Steel Hour; Broadway version follows the same year (Tony nomination)
A Face in the Crowd (dir. Elia Kazan) — film debut as Lonesome Rhodes
No Time for Sergeants — film version
What It Was, Was Football LP released on Capitol — collected monologues
The Andy Griffith Show premieres on CBS — runs 1960–68, 249 episodes
I Love to Tell the Story — Grammy Award for Best Southern, Country, or Bluegrass Gospel Album
Awarded the Presidential Medal of Freedom
Died 3 July at his home on Roanoke Island, North Carolina, age 86

accolades: 'What It Was, Was Football' (1954) — No. 9 on the Billboard pop chart; effectively the first comedy monologue hit of the LP era
Tony Award nomination, No Time for Sergeants (1956)
Grammy Award, Best Southern, Country, or Bluegrass Gospel Album (I Love to Tell the Story, 1996)
Inducted into the North Carolina Music Hall of Fame; the Andy Griffith Museum in Mount Airy preserves his career artifacts

specials.t: What It Is, Is Andy Griffith (Andy's Greatest Comedy Monologues & Old-Timey Songs)
specials.plat: Capitol/UMe — career compilation of the 1953–58 monologue catalogue
Colonial Records; reissued by Capitol Records December 1953
